seo.title: PrimaSTEM | How It Works - Product Details
seo.description: Complete technical overview of the PrimaSTEM educational robot. Components, commands, drawing, specifications..

hero.title: How PrimaSTEM works
hero.videoCaption: Video recorded with a prototype. Final product may differ.

steps.text: Place tokens left to right. Green LED = accepted. The board speaks the command.
Press "Start." The robot executes. LEDs show which command is running.
Check the result. Wrong? Find the error, swap a token, run again.

cards.title: Ladybug robot
items: approximately 2 hours battery, depending on usage

commands.title: Commands
commands.text:
• Movement & Program: Forward, Backward, Left, Right, Random, Function, Pause.
  • Repeat: 2–999, Random repeat 1–6.
  • Mathematics: +, −, ×, ÷, √, ^
  • Numbers: 1–999
  • System: Default step size, calibration.

drawing.title: Drawing
drawing.text:
Slip in a marker and every move leaves a line. Forward 50, Right 144, Repeat 5 makes a star; add an arithmetic progression and the star becomes a spiral.

The robot works out the angles and distances as it goes, drawing real geometry on paper. A plain rolling robot with no screen cannot match that precision, and the child sees the result of their program right there on the page.

maps.title: Works with the maps and lesson plans you already have
maps.text:
PrimaSTEM adapts to the content of other educational robots.

Step size is adjustable - 10, 12, 15, 25 cm. Any grid map works. No new materials needed.

There's a ready-made curriculum in the box for the little ones, and lesson plans built for another robot work on PrimaSTEM too.

openPlatform.title: Open platform
paragraphs: Every token is a standard NFC tag (13.56 MHz). Write any code using a free phone app. Create your own numbers, loops or arithmetic operations.
NFC tags on the playing field - the robot plays audio when it drives over them. Language cards, musical fields, story quests.

voiceFeedback.title: Voice, words and music
paragraphs: The board speaks each token as it's placed. Helps children who can't read yet and children with visual impairments.

Roll it onto a word card and it reads the word aloud, around ninety in all, from house to forest to cinema. It names the letters of the alphabet, plays musical notes, and reads arithmetic out loud. The same robot that teaches coding and counting brings in speech and music along the way.
Available in 17 languages: English, Français, Русский, Українська, Deutsch, Español, Italiano, Português (Brasil), Nederlands, Norsk, Polski, Svenska, Türkçe, Dansk, Català, 日本語, עברית.

rows.label: Battery

rows.label: Audio
rows.value: Voice feedback in 17 languages + sound samples
